doomsayer.description: Overthinks every dark outcome—feels everyone's pain, predicts collapse. Withdraws completely, ghosts without warning.
doomsayer.recovery: Breathe. Write it out. Call someone real.

manipulator.description: Uses deep empathy as leverage—reads minds, twists trust. Intuition turned against the people you love.
manipulator.recovery: Own the manipulation. Apologize directly. Let go of control.

tyrant.description: Micromanages everything into submission—'my way or the project dies.' Cold control masquerading as competence.
tyrant.recovery: Ask, don't command. One person's input might save the mission.

nihilist.description: Sees no point in building anything—'why construct what entropy destroys?' Cynical shutdown disguised as realism.
nihilist.recovery: Build anyway. Meaning comes after momentum, not before.

martyr.description: Drowns in other people's suffering while abandoning their own—absorbs wounds and calls it devotion.
martyr.recovery: Your pain is real—but so is your life. Put yourself on the list.

escapist.description: Retreats so deep into inner worlds that reality becomes optional. Disappears when it gets real.
escapist.recovery: Come back. The real world needs your tenderness.

paralyst.description: Analysis loops forever—never commits, never decides, never lands. Brilliant in theory, absent in practice.
paralyst.recovery: Ship the imperfect version. Done beats perfect every time.

detacher.description: Severs emotional connection when overwhelmed. Goes cold without knowing they've gone cold.
detacher.recovery: Name what you're feeling. Even 'I don't know' is a start.

savior.description: Needs to rescue everyone—even those who didn't ask. Resentment calcifies when people refuse to be saved.
savior.recovery: Let people fail. That's how they grow.

puppeteer.description: Guides people 'for their own good'—but really toward your preferred outcome. Warm-faced control.
puppeteer.recovery: Ask what they actually want. Then let them have it.

ghost.description: Disappears at peak intimacy—flits to the next exciting thing before depth is required.
ghost.recovery: Stay. The discomfort is where the real connection lives.

chaos-agent.description: Sets fire to stable situations unconsciously. Pattern: create spark, watch burn, feel bad, repeat.
chaos-agent.recovery: Before igniting—ask: am I bored, or is this genuinely broken?

steamroller.description: Crushes dissent disguised as decisiveness. Everyone's opinion is noise until proven otherwise.
steamroller.recovery: Slow down. The fastest path to the goal includes the people.

workaholic.description: Replaces connection with achievement. Builds an empire and populates it with no one.
workaholic.recovery: Log off. The empire can wait. The people cannot.

contrarian.description: Argues against every position including their own—not to discover truth but to avoid committing to one.
contrarian.recovery: Pick a side. Commit. You can always update your position.

dodger.description: Uses wit to deflect accountability. Faster with a reframe than an apology.
dodger.recovery: Say you're sorry first. The clever reframe can come later.

people-pleaser.description: Says yes until resentment becomes silence becomes distance. Martyrdom without announcement.
people-pleaser.recovery: Your 'no' is not rejection—it's honesty. Try it once.

gatekeeper.description: Protects tradition past its expiration date—mistakes familiarity for wisdom, comfort for safety.
gatekeeper.recovery: Ask: is this serving people, or just protecting your comfort?

crusher.description: Facts only—no room for feeling, nuance, or context. Technically correct, emotionally devastating.
crusher.recovery: Facts without feeling aren't wisdom. Slow down.

lone-wolf.description: Refuses to delegate because no one will do it right. Collapses under the weight of being permanently needed.
lone-wolf.recovery: Hand it off. Trust one person. See what happens.

approval-addict.description: Self-worth collapses without constant validation. Manages perception at the cost of honest truth.
approval-addict.recovery: Do one thing today that no one will see. That's your real self.

gossip.description: Processes anxiety through social triangulation. Shares too much about others to feel momentarily connected.
gossip.recovery: Process it privately. Write it, don't say it.

authoritarian.description: Enforces rules past their purpose—order for order's sake, not for people's. The system is never wrong.
authoritarian.recovery: Rules serve people. When they don't—break them.

dismisser.description: Invalidates emotional experience as 'irrational.' Efficient with problems, brutal with feelings.
dismisser.recovery: Say 'I hear you' before anything else. Meaning it helps.

avoider.description: Disappears from conflict without explanation—leaves people wondering what they did wrong.
avoider.recovery: Send the text. One sentence. Just start.

slow-burn.description: Resists gently until resentment calcifies into silence. By the time anyone knows, they're already gone.
slow-burn.recovery: Say it now, while the feeling is still warm.

shutdown.description: Goes emotionally unreachable under pressure—becomes unavailable and doesn't understand why people are upset.
shutdown.recovery: You don't have to feel it publicly. But feel it somewhere.

reckless.description: Risk logic overrides consequence logic. 'It'll probably be fine' covers a multitude of preventable disasters.
reckless.recovery: Pause for a five-second risk scan. That's all it takes.

performer.description: When things get heavy, puts on a show instead of processing. The performance covers the wound perfectly.
performer.recovery: Drop the act for five minutes. Who's actually there?

reactive.description: Acts from raw emotion in the moment, deals with consequences later—often too late to repair the damage.
reactive.recovery: Ten seconds before responding. Just ten. Then decide.

opportunist.description: Uses charm to extract advantage. Reads the angle before reading the room—relationships as transactions.
opportunist.recovery: Play the long game. Reputation outlasts every angle.

reckless.description: Moves so fast the collateral damage becomes someone else's problem. Usually is. Usually permanent.
reckless.recovery: Slow down long enough to see the debris field.
